title: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice
wikitext:
[]: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Ari » Sun Apr 19, 2015 1:41 am so, i'm cosplaying Rei and i want to get it just perfect. i got a wig that i like and it is also in the right color, but it is 1.) too long and 2.) a bit curly. so, i will probably use hot water to straighten it, but how should i cut i to Ayanami Rei's haircut? i've never cut hair before except for trimming my own bangs. Here's the wig: http://i.imgur.com/s9Exv0c.jpg as for the makeup, i'm probably going to use pink lipstick + pink blush and white eyeliner on my lower waterline. i have an eye checkup soon so i have a chance to maybe get prescription colored contacts if i want to go that far, lol. i wonder if the price is worth it and for the actual costume, i just got one from bodyline and i'm wearing white loafers with thigh highs. idk if i'll wear bandages or how i'd wear them is there anything i should change for the cosplay (or anything additional i should do)? i want it to look as good as it can

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Apr 19, 2015 8:48 am Ari wrote: as for the makeup, i'm probably going to use pink lipstick + pink blush and white eyeliner on my lower waterline.

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Ari » Sun Apr 19, 2015 9:11 am Guy Nacks wrote: Rei does not wear lipstick and is as white as a ghost. No makeup unless it's making your skin tone less pigmented.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Apr 19, 2015 8:03 pm I think the best thing is to ask folks who have already done the cosplay for pointers. You could try these places out to find someone who's done a Rei cosplay. I don't browse the sites, just heard of them. https://worldcosplay.net/ http://www.cosplay.com/ http://www.acparadise.com/

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 19, 2015 10:12 pm I've seen a few really good Rei cosplays where they used red contacts to complete the effect. Cool stuff. Guy Nacks: In any kind of cosplay/acting/etc, a little bit of makeup is actually good to help enhance your natural features more.

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Sat Apr 25, 2015 3:12 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I've seen a few really good Rei cosplays where they used red contacts to complete the effect. Cool stuff. Guy Nacks: In any kind of cosplay/acting/etc, a little bit of makeup is actually good to help enhance your natural features more.

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Apr 25, 2015 4:00 pm Convincing, but I noticed something in your original post. . . Ari wrote: so, i'm cosplaying Rei and i want to get it just perfect.

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Ari » Sat Apr 25, 2015 4:37 pm Stryker wrote: Convincing, but I noticed something in your original post. . . What do you consider perfect: An accurate recreation of Rei Ayanami, or an accurate depiction of Rei Ayanami in real life? The former philosophy I have no doubt you will get right; you will most certainly be recognized as Rei Ayanami with that wig. The latter philosophy is going need some serious reconsideration; if you take into account the fact that Rei lives in poorer living conditions, likely does not take good care of her hygiene (other than what is necessary to be healthy, of course), her hair would likely look more messy than you let your wig be. Figure out what you want.

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 25, 2015 5:40 pm Stryker wrote: likely does not take good care of her hygiene (other than what is necessary to be healthy, of course)

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Apr 25, 2015 5:47 pm Stryker wrote: Rei...likely does not take good care of her hygiene (other than what is necessary to be healthy, of course).

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 25, 2015 5:50 pm For what it's worth, Sadamoto stated (somewhere) that Rei "smells like a hospital", but part of that is probably because she's in them so often!

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Ari » Sat Apr 25, 2015 7:06 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Rule Number 1 of Cons: Always shower before (and during if you can). Always. Rei's hair being a little unkempt would look cute for sure. You could always do a casual look she wears in the artbooks etc (check MiniTokyo gallery for some images) so it'd be closer to something you'd actually wear instead of just a plugsuit or school uniform.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Apr 25, 2015 8:15 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: For what it's worth, Sadamoto stated (somewhere) that Rei "smells like a hospital", but part of that is probably because she's in them so often!

[]:             Quote Post by Shoujo Kakumei Asuka » Sat Apr 25, 2015 9:30 pm I know this sounds odd to say of someone who left blood-soaked bandages around her room, but Rei never struck me as someone who was that disorderly. She probably does not put as much effort in her appearance as Asuka does, but I imagine she still keeps her health, as Guy Nacks says. I'd recommend the contacts as well, if you want to go the extra mile!

[]:             Quote Post by Ari » Sat Apr 25, 2015 11:53 pm Shoujo Kakumei Asuka wrote: I know this sounds odd to say of someone who left blood-soaked bandages around her room, but Rei never struck me as someone who was that disorderly. She probably does not put as much effort in her appearance as Asuka does, but I imagine she still keeps her health, as Guy Nacks says. I'd recommend the contacts as well, if you want to go the extra mile!

[]:             Quote Post by TheAdmiral » Sun Apr 26, 2015 5:36 pm I guess it depends on how comfortable you are in costume and how long you'll be in it. I'd not recommend the bandages just for the blind spot that it'll create. Do keep us updated with your progress though, I think your wig looks quite good as-is.

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Apr 26, 2015 5:52 pm Off-topic  SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Mon Apr 27, 2015 3:59 pm Stryker wrote: Being stuck with one eye messes with you binocular vision; you will have a hard time picking things up, because your depth perception will not be as acute.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Apr 27, 2015 4:18 pm One thing I might suggest? Skip the plugsuit/school uniform and do Rei in casual clothes (matching a Sadamoto illustration would do the trick). It's so rare to see Rei cosplayed in normal clothes, and the problem with that is that making either the school uniform or (especially) the plugsuit look good is close to impossible. But the wig you have is very good -- it looks like natural hair dyed blue vs. the artificial monstrosities that show up in so many Rei cosplays, and if you get the contacts everyone will know who you are cosplaying no matter what you're wearing. So, I say break with tradition and play Rei in her "I'm a normal girl" mode!

[]: Re: Ayanami Rei cosplay advice?             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 27, 2015 8:12 pm I definitely second this, like I recommended back here: Sailor Star Dust wrote: You could always do a casual look she wears in the artbooks etc (check MiniTokyo gallery for some images) so it'd be closer to something you'd actually wear instead of just a plugsuit or school uniform.
